title: <yeet> element: source + transport + format
description: Implement the <yeet> custom element. Attributes: source= (selector for the projected element), transport= (transport name from registry). Children: <yeet-format> elements declaring offered serialization formats (innerHTML, json-patch, morphdom, binary). On connected: look up transport, connect, observe source via MutationObserver, serialize mutations, send. On disconnected: close via yee-goeth graceful teardown. Emits yee-there during connection setup.

title: YeetTransport / Connection / YeetPayload interface contract
description: Land the core TypeScript interfaces: YeetTransport (connect(): Promise<Connection>), Connection (send(payload), messages(): AsyncIterable, close(): Promise<void>), YeetPayload (format, body, optional patch). Lives in src/contract.ts. No transport implementations in this ticket — those are per-transport. The contract is what transports implement and what <yeet> consumes.

title: cast transport: delegates to cast-yee
description: Implement the 'cast' transport as a thin adapter over cast-yee. The first concrete transport; proves the contract. Lives either in yeetable core (since cast-yee is the canonical case) or as a separate package depending on whether we want yeetable to ship with zero transport deps. Probably the latter — register cast as an optional peer.

title: Transport registry: name -> transport factory
description: Implement the transport registry. Transports register by name (cast, postmessage, popup, webrtc, broadcastchannel, sharedworker, sw). <yeet transport='X'> looks up X in the registry and instantiates. Includes the registration API and the default empty registry.

title: Serialization formats: innerHTML, json-patch, morphdom, binary
description: Epic: land the per-format serialization helpers. <yeet-format name='X'> declares one. Initial formats: innerHTML (current cast-mixer behavior, blunt), json-patch (RFC 6902), morphdom (diff-then-patch), binary (raw bytes, for arrow-data / moq frames). Each format is a small package registering with a global format registry.

title: broadcastchannel transport: same-origin multi-tab sync
description: BroadcastChannel transport: simplest same-origin multi-tab projection. Zero setup, zero configuration. Useful as the default demo transport and as a local dev tool.

title: popup transport: window.open + BroadcastChannel pingback
description: popup transport: opens a new window with window.open, communicates via BroadcastChannel (since direct refs break across navigations). Useful for popping a view out into its own window while keeping it live-synced.

title: postmessage transport: iframe/window projection
description: postMessage transport: target is an iframe or window reference. Payloads are structured-cloned. Useful for previewing page state in a dev tool, projecting component state into a sandbox, etc.

title: sw transport: project into service worker namespace
description: Service worker transport: page projects element state into the SW's namespace, where it becomes HTTP-addressable. This is the flightcomp/macro-refid pattern. Lives in yeetable as one transport among many — flightcomp and macro-refid consume.

title: sharedworker transport: project page state into a SharedWorker
description: SharedWorker transport: page(s) project state into a worker; worker aggregates and re-emits. Pairs with flightcomp's SW model. Useful for cross-tab coordination without a server.

title: webrtc transport: DataChannel-based peer-to-peer projection
description: WebRTC transport: peer-to-peer DataChannel. Heaviest setup (signaling required) but enables viewer-to-viewer fan-out without a server. Pairs interestingly with moq as a relay alternative.
